text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic excision of a para-ovarian cyst, using minimally invasive techniques to remove the cyst from the surrounding ovarian tissue.

The surgeon introduces the secondary trocar under direct vision, carefully avoiding damage to the bowel and other important structures. The bowel is gently pushed away from the operative site to prevent injury during the procedure.
The uterus with bilateral fallopian tubes is visible. Gentle pressure is applied to the left fallopian tube to expose the para-ovarian cyst adjacent to it.

The para-ovarian cyst, appearing large and simple, is visible. A small portion of the cyst is cauterized to prevent bleeding at the entry point. The surgeon makes an incision in the cyst wall using scissors, creating a small nick inside the cyst.

The surgeon drains the fluid from the para-ovarian cyst using a suction cannula, completely emptying the cyst. The remaining cyst wall is excised with scissors after achieving hemostasis with cautery to prevent further bleeding.
The surgeon secures hemostasis around the para-ovarian cyst using cautery before completely excising the cyst wall with surgical scissors.
